title: Resinous, Melted, Shocked French Foie-Gras Quiche
description: A French quiche dish featuring rich foie-gras, crumbly texture, and a hint of resinous flavor. This quiche makes for a unique and flavorful breakfast or brunch option.

ingredients.name: foie-gras
ingredients.notes: fresh, high-quality foie-gras is essential for this dish

ingredients.name: onion
ingredients.notes: thinly sliced, caramelized to bring out sweetness

ingredients.name: butter
ingredients.notes: unsalted, melted to infuse into foie-gras

ingredients.name: eggs
ingredients.notes: fresh, large eggs for quiche filling

ingredients.name: heavy cream
ingredients.notes: fresh, heavy cream to add richness to quice

ingredients.name: cheese
ingredients.notes: soft, mild cheese to balance foie-gras taste

ingredients.name: milk
ingredients.notes: optional, for extra creaminess in quiche

ingredients.name: baking powder
ingredients.notes: to help quiche rise and crisp up

instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit (190 degrees Celsius).
Roll out the pie crust to a thickness of 1/8 inch and place it in a 9-inch round baking dish.
Melt the butter in a skillet over medium heat and saut7 onion until caramelized, about 10 minutes.
In a separate bowl, whisk together eggs, heavy cream, cheese, and baking powder. Add the cooked onion and foie-gras to the egg mixture and stir to combine.
Pour the egg mixture into the pie crust and smooth out the top.
Bake the quiche in the preheated oven for 35-40 minutes, or until the edges are golden brown and the center is set.
Remove the quiche from the oven and let it cool on a wire rack for 5 minutes.
Transfer the quiche to a plate or serving dish and serve warm, garnished with fresh herbs or edible flowers if desired.
For the egg wash, whisk together egg and milk (if using) in a small bowl until smooth. Brush the egg wash over the pie crust to give it a golden glaze.
If desired, garnish the quiche with additional fresh herbs or edible flowers before serving
